description_long: This helicopter features a futuristic, angular design with a predominantly green and black color scheme, accented by metallic and weathered textures. The body is compact and streamlined, with two large, forward-swept wing structures that house long, cylindrical gun-like appendages. The central fuselage is robust, with a rounded, glass-like cockpit at the front and a blue-tinted engine or thruster at the rear. The surface is marked by a grid-like pattern and some visible wear, suggesting heavy use or combat readiness. The helicopter lacks traditional rotor blades and tail rotors, instead appearing to utilize advanced propulsion technology. Its overall appearance is aggressive and high-tech, suitable for urban tactical operations.
description: A compact, futuristic green and black helicopter with forward-swept wings, gun-like appendages, and a streamlined, armored body. The design is angular and aggressive, with a glassy cockpit and blue rear thruster.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, glassy cockpit, and symmetrical forward-swept wings with gun-like extensions.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the flat, rectangular body, the length of the wing appendages, and the absence of visible rotors.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the blue-tinted thruster, the angular wing structures, and the enclosed, armored rear section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the same flat body and extended appendages, with visible surface wear.
attribute_landing_skids: No traditional landing skids; the helicopter appears to have a flat, integrated base for landing, likely made of reinforced metal or composite material.
attribute_cockpit_windows: One large, rounded, glass-like cockpit window at the front, slightly tinted and seamlessly integrated into the fuselage with no visible framing.
